ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Scale of the amount of opioid pain medication prescribed when the participant was discharged from the hospital; as part of the A2CPS - Opioid & Pain Control Satisfaction (OPCS)
Notes: 1 = Much less than I needed; 2 = Less than I needed; 3 = About right; 4 = More than I needed; 5 = Much more than I needed; 6 = Not applicable, I was not prescribed opioids after discharge

ElementDescription: Scale of the extent to which the participant is satisfied with pain control after their surgery; as part of the A2CPS - Opioid & Pain Control Satisfaction (OPCS)
Scale of the extent to which the participant describes their pain control since surgery compared to expected pain control; as part of the A2CPS - Opioid & Pain Control Satisfaction (OPCS)
Notes: 1 = Much worse than you expected; 2 = Worse than you expected; 3 = About what you expected; 4 = Better than you expected; 5 = Much better than you expected
